programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The jury got further details into the murder investigation of Rosemarie Essa. On the stand, was the detective who went all the way to Greece to corroborate Yazeed's location as a wanted U.S. fugitive living in Lebanon. Detective Dennis Matejcici confirmed that he interviewed Yazeed's alleged fixer all the way in Greece about where Yazeed was living while evading the law. After this homicide detective's testimony, the state of Ohio formally rested their case in chief.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Yazeed Essa's defense team began their case on the importance of taking calcium as a person ages. At the time of her death, Rosemarie was taking a daily dose of calcium supplements along with prenatal vitamins. She was trying to get pregnant with her and Yazeed's third child. Next, the defendant's co-worker and former employee told jurors a different side to his relationship with his longtime mistress.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Yazeed Essa's former employee testified in his defense case. Ron Onesko was under cross-examination testifying about Yazeed's extramarital affair with his mistress, Marguerita Montanez that was happening in the weeks leading up to Rosemarie's murder. Next to the witness stand was even more former employees of the defendant. It was all about how serious Yazeed's secret relationship with his longtime mistress really was just months leading up to Rosemarie's poisoning death.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: A secret love affair became the focus in the Defense's case. Yazeed Essa's lawyers brought in employees who confirmed the sexual affair he carried on outside of his marriage to Rosemarie. Next, the defendant's brother's attorney took the stand. When Firas Essa returned to the stand, he pleaded the Fifth for lying under oath about a confession Yazeed made while he was in jail in Cyprus awaiting extradition back to the U.S.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a rash of home invasions plagued the Detroit area. Each time, masked men disguised as police officers broke in with assault rifles and stole money and jewelry. But when one homeowner shot back, the identity of the intruders was revealed.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William appeared to be a friendly gardener, but his battered van hid a horrifying secret. It served as a mobile prison where he killed women before dumping their bodies in an isolated area he chillingly called his "garden."

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, mutilated and killed at least eight women in the US.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A routine traffic stop revealed a body in Joel's car, unraveling a long string of murders targeting vulnerable women. The discovery ended his violent reign and gave investigators answers long out of reach.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Victor Farrant is a convicted rapist who murdered his former girlfriend in 1996 and attempted to murder another woman after he was released from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert gained trust easily, slipping into the lives of vulnerable women and girls across countries. For decades, he manipulated, stalked and attacked, leaving a trail of victims in his wake until investigators finally exposed his pattern.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A family's worst nightmare when 16-year-old Becky Watts goes missing from her home and is found dead a few days' later, murdered by her own stepbrother.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.desc: Billy Dunlop killed Julie Hogg in 1989 but was acquitted after two failed trials. However, due to a change in the UK law in 2005, Dunlop was convicted and sentenced to seventeen years in prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a residential community saw anything the day one of their neighbors was brutally murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carried manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who suffered from mental illness is found dead. Was it due to natural causes, suicide or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1971, John List left a note with the bodies of his family members in his mansion before he disappeared.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An infant was rushed to the emergency room with serious breathing problems. Within months, there were more than 30 cases. Doctors searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Shortly after daybreak in Vancouver, British Columbia, a fire was set in a dumpster. No one saw either the arsonist or the fire, and it burned for hours in the deserted parking lot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A drive-by shooting leaves one man dead and another seriously wounded. Cell phone calls and shell casings point to a suspect, but authorities are unable to place him at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-year-old boy was rushed to the hospital suffering from brain seizures and breathing difficulties.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Prompted by news of a flight attendant's brutal murder, the son of a previous victim contacts police.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspect's former lover comes forward with a tale of murder, mutilation and cremation, but there's no way to test the story's validity until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronologist conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared and her husband became the prime suspect especially after police found a huge bloodstain on the carpet of their bedroom. Complex DNA testing proved it was the wife's blood. Now all they had to do was find her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police suspect Dr. John Boyle in the disappearance of his wife when he buys a new house with a woman pretending to be his spouse. The police later find a body in the basement of his new house.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace. Even though foul play is suspected, the leads don't pan out and the trail turns cold. Twenty years would pass before police are able to link a clump of hair discovered in an isolated area with the missing woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a search for the killer of two teenagers in Texas, a behavioral profile led to suspect Jason Massey, and hard science proved the profile was correct. Massey was executed by lethal injection on April 3, 2001.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men called police to report the same murder. The investigation uncovered more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a policeman's house goes up in flames, experts suspect arson. The officer whose home was torched arrests a low-level drug dealer, who confesses. But then the suspect vanishes.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people witness a truck driving over an unconscious man and flee into a nearby bar, but when the police turn up, the truck, the man and the witnesses are gone.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael "Mick" Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.desc: When a pipe bomb rips through a rural home, killing a young man and seriously injuring his mother, detectives hope that the remnants of a mailing label will lead them to a killer.
